prompt:
Add two transport-economics rules to @prune/cache-habits and wire them into the linter alongside the existing CH-001..CH-012 rules:

CH-013 "transport_regression": fires when a session that had been on a STATEFUL transport (server retains history) transitions to a STATELESS one, which silently re-sends the whole accumulated history every turn. Price the re-sent history at the model's input rate; when the history token size is unknown, the finding still fires but its cost MUST be null (never a fabricated number).

CH-014 "stateful_transport_advisor": advises when a long-running stateless-transport session would benefit from a stateful transport. Contingent advice — it must NOT claim a dollar saving. It must not fire below a minimum turn floor, when the re-communicated history is below the minimum cacheable prefix, when history size is unknown, or when the transport isn't stateless.

On the transition turn itself the snapshot still reads 'stateful', so CH-013 fires and CH-014 must short-circuit — the two rules never double-fire on the same turn.
notes: Reference: feat(cache-habits) CH-013/CH-014 2026-06-07. Feature-against-test-spec; oracle restricted to the cache-habits package.
